channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: ICC Appeals Chamber Affirms Jurisdiction Over Duterte Case
item.description: On April 22, 2026, the International Criminal Court (ICC) Appeals Chamber ruled on former Philippine President Rodrigo Duterte’s challenge to the court’s jurisdiction in his crimes against humanity ca...

item.title: How a Reddit community is helping people embrace baldness
item.description: The article describes the online community r/bald on Reddit, a space where people who are losing their hair or considering shaving their heads come together to encourage one another. The subreddit’s s...

item.title: Developing a framework to assess cultural and spiritual loss in Native Title compensation cases
item.description: Australian courts are currently facing a complex legal challenge: how to properly value the cultural and spiritual harm suffered by Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander Peoples when their traditional...

item.title: Starmer confirms discussions over potential diplomatic role for aide amid wider political row
item.description: Sir Keir Starmer has acknowledged that discussions took place within Downing Street regarding a possible diplomatic role for his former communications chief, Matthew Doyle, though he insisted that no ...

item.title: Storage Wars personality Darrell Sheets dies aged 67 in Arizona
item.description: Darrell Sheets, best known for his appearances on the reality television series Storage Wars, has died at the age of 67 in Arizona. According to a statement from the Lake Havasu Police Department, off...

item.title: Panel Debates Fallout from Mandelson Vetting Scandal and Starmer's Leadership
item.description: A Guardian panel discusses the escalating scandal over Peter Mandelson's appointment as US ambassador despite failing security vetting linked to his Jeffrey Epstein ties. Olly Robbins, the sacked Fore...

item.title: Ontario reverses purchase of $28.9M private jet after public backlash
item.description: The Ontario government has reversed its controversial purchase of a $28.9 million Bombardier Challenger 650 private jet, selling it back to the manufacturer less than a week after the deal became publ...

item.title: Video shows black bear charging at hiker on Mount Wilson; no injuries reported as encounters with bears increase in California
item.description: A video captured on Mount Wilson in the Angeles National Forest shows a black bear charging toward a hiker, though no one was injured. The clip, posted on Instagram by Eric Chiu, depicts the hiker app...

item.title: Iran acknowledges Pakistan’s mediation as ceasefire tensions with US persist
item.description: Iran has appreciated Pakistan’s diplomatic efforts to mediate between Tehran and Washington amid an ongoing and fragile ceasefire situation. Iranian Foreign Ministry spokesman Esmaeil Baqaei stated th...

item.title: Prince Harry leaves Meghan Markle's Her Best Life wellness retreat in Sydney shortly after her Q&A session
item.description: Prince Harry, 41, was seen quickly exiting the stage area at his wife Meghan Markle's Her Best Life wellness retreat held at the Intercontinental Coogee in Sydney on Friday. A video captured him risin...

item.title: Carney rejects additional concessions ahead of potential U.S.-Canada trade negotiations
item.description: Prime Minister Mark Carney stated that Canada will not offer further concessions to the United States as a condition for entering new trade negotiations. Speaking to reporters before a Liberal caucus ...

item.title: Shortage of Digital Skills in Public Sector Hinders Mexico's Digital Government Progress
item.description: Mexico continues to launch new digital platforms, apps for government procedures, transparency portals, and citizen service systems every year. However, a critical structural challenge threatens these...

item.title: Russian Messaging App MAX Launched in Tanzania to Improve Connectivity
item.description: Tanzania has officially introduced a new messaging application called MAX, developed in Russia, as part of efforts to expand digital communication options in the country. The launch event took place a...

item.title: Inquiry Report on 2025 Election Incidents Set for Submission to President Samia
item.description: The Independent Commission of Inquiry formed to investigate the breach of peace during and after Tanzania’s October 2025 General Election is expected to submit its final report to President Samia Sulu...

item.title: Practical eco-friendly gadgets to reduce home energy and water use in 2026
item.description: The article highlights a range of eco-friendly gadgets designed to help households reduce energy consumption, conserve water, and lower their environmental footprint. It emphasizes that sustainable li...

item.title: Inquiry launched into DISCOs’ costly off-grid electricity purchases violating merit order rules
item.description: Amid rising electricity prices and ongoing public complaints about load-shedding in Pakistan, the government has revealed that several Distribution Companies (DISCOs) have been purchasing electricity ...

item.title: Search teams recover one crew member after cargo ship capsizes during typhoon near Northern Mariana Islands
item.description: Authorities searching for six missing crew members from the cargo ship Mariana, which overturned during Super Typhoon Sinlaku near the Northern Mariana Islands, have recovered the body of one individu...

item.title: International Criminal Court confirms authority to proceed with Duterte case
item.description: The International Criminal Court (ICC) has upheld its jurisdiction over the case involving former Philippine President Rodrigo Duterte, according to the report. This development means that the tribuna...

item.title: Harvey Weinstein faces retrial over 2013 rape allegation involving aspiring actress Jessica Mann
item.description: In New York, the retrial of former Hollywood film producer Harvey Weinstein continued with prosecutors presenting fresh arguments that he allegedly used his influence in the entertainment industry to ...

item.title: Islamabad eases road closures in twin cities, but Red Zone remains under strict security amid foreign delegation visit uncertainty
item.description: Islamabad’s security situation saw partial easing as several major routes in the twin cities were reopened to the public after being closed for days due to anticipated visits by foreign delegations. H...
